mainEntity.name: How much does an XF engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: An XF engine replacement costs £3,000-£5,800 for the Ingenium range (X260) and £4,500-£7,500 for the AJ126 V6 (X250) — supply-only, plus 10-20 hours of labour at £65-£95/hr independent rates. Total fitted cost typically lands at £4,000-£9,500 depending on engine and generation. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised estimate.

mainEntity.name: Which XF engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ200P / 204PT petrol Ingenium is the most reliable XF engine in our data — the water pump is the only meaningful weak point, and replacement costs are sensible (£400-£800). The diesel Ingenium (AJ200D) is also solid from 2019 onward, but the petrol has the cleaner reliability record overall.

mainEntity.name: Which XF engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine outright, but the AJ126 3.0 V6 Supercharged (X250, 2008-2015) carries two genuine caveats: cooling system failures (plastic crossover pipes crack with age) and pre-2013 timing chain tensioner wear. Buy a 2013+ car with documented coolant system work, or walk away. The pre-2019 diesel Ingenium also carries a timing chain tensioner risk — buy 2019+.

mainEntity.name: Is the XF timing chain problem real?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — but it depends on the engine. The AJ126 V6 (X250, pre-2013) and AJ200D diesel Ingenium (X260, pre-2019) both have documented timing chain tensioner issues. The revised 2013 chain tensioner on the AJ126 and the 2019 redesign on the Ingenium diesel largely resolved the problem. Cold-start rattle is the primary warning — investigate immediately.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an XF engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends entirely on generation and vehicle value. An X260 replacement (£3,000-£5,800) against an £8,000-£25,000 car sits at 30-50% — safe. An X250 AJ126 replacement (£4,500-£7,500) against a £4,000-£12,000 car can approach 60-80% on sub-£7k examples — borderline. If replacement cost exceeds roughly 60% of what the car is worth, get a scrap valuation before committing.

mainEntity.name: Which XF generation should I buy?

acceptedAnswer.text: Buy a 2019+ X260 with the AJ200P petrol Ingenium. It's the cleanest reliability profile in the XF range, replacement costs are sensible, and you avoid the pre-2019 diesel timing chain caveat entirely. If you're considering an X250, buy a 2013+ car with documented cooling system work and be prepared for V6 running costs.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my XF engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Your XF's engine code is stamped on the engine block (visible near the front), on the VIN plate (character 4-7), and in your vehicle documentation. For X260, look for '204DTD' (diesel) or '204PT' (petrol) on the block. For X250, look for 'AJ126' (V6 petrol) or the TDV6 codes. Our diagnostic calculator can identify your engine from your VIN or registration.

mainEntity.name: XF vs XE — which is more reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Both carry a Safe verdict in our data, but for different reasons. The XF X260 benefits from the modern Ingenium range — the petrol is genuinely reliable, and the diesel is improved from 2019 onward. The XE is the more affordable entry point, but the XF offers better refinement and a more spacious cabin. Read our full comparison here: /compare/xf-vs-xe/

hero.tagPill: Jaguar • XF • 2008-2024 • 2 Generations • AJ126 V6 • AJ200D Ingenium • AJ200P
hero.h1: XF Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every XF generation, from the 2008 X250 to the 2024 X260, covered in full — engine codes, known failures, replacement costs, and the one thing every buyer needs to know. Backed by 276 real UK XF X260 engine enquiries and 178 XF X250 enquiries in 2025 [JAG-VERIFIED].

metrics.ourCall: ✅ The XF rule: generation is everything — the X260's modern Ingenium range makes replacement viable across almost every example, while the X250's AJ126 V6 can approach the watch threshold on sub-£7k cars
AJ200P / 204PT — petrol Ingenium's water pump replacement is the only meaningful weak point
AJ126 3.0 V6 SC — cooling system failures and pre-2013 chain tensioner wear are well-documented
2019+ X260 with the AJ200P petrol Ingenium — the cleanest reliability profile in the range [JAG-VERIFIED]

ownershipVerdict.oneLineVerdict: The XF is the executive saloon that finally fixed Jaguar's reliability reputation — but only if you buy the right generation, and the line is 2015.

generations.subHeadline: Two distinct generations, two very different ownership stories — one you buy, one you assess carefully.

cards.title: X250 (2008-2015)
cards.verdict: The X250 is a handsome, well-built executive saloon with a genuine caveat — the AJ126 V6's cooling system failures and pre-2013 chain tensioner wear can turn a £5k car into a £12k bill. Buy a 2013+ car with documented coolant system work, or walk away.

cards.title: X260 (2015-2024)
cards.verdict: The X260 is the XF Jaguar always meant to build — the Ingenium petrol is genuinely reliable, the diesel's pre-2019 tensioner risk is well-understood, and replacement costs sit comfortably within vehicle values. The safest used executive saloon Jaguar makes.

cards.title: Comparison sub-block:
cards.meta: The dividing line: 2015 is the line that matters. Pre-2015 X250s carry the AJ126 V6 with its cooling system and chain tensioner caveats. 2015-on X260s get the Ingenium range — the petrol is clean, the diesel is improved from 2019, and replacement economics are far more favourable.

engineDatabase.h2: XF Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the XF — verified codes, fuel types, power outputs, reliability ratings, and real 2025 enquiry volumes.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: The AJ200D (276 enquiries) and AJ126 (178 enquiries) ratings are 75-90% confidence — based on 454 total [JAG-VERIFIED] enquiries plus cross-referenced specialist data from the Known Failure Patterns table. The AJ200P and 306DT ratings are Expert-knowledge tier — documented failure patterns and specialist interviews, not proprietary volume data. Data note (internal): Correction applied: Original sheet listed AJ133 (5.0 V8) as an XF engine. The AJ133 was fitted to XF-R models and is correctly listed in the engine table — retained. No corrections needed otherwise — source data matched live verification against Jaguar technical specifications and Parkers/Autocar sources.

commonProblems.subHeadline: The six most common XF issues by enquiry volume — what fails, which engines it affects, and what it costs to fix.

problems.issue: AJ126 Cooling System & Plastic Crossover Pipe Failure
problems.description: The 3.0 V6's plastic coolant crossover pipes are a well-documented weak point — they crack with age, and these engines cannot tolerate overheating without head-gasket damage. Most common on pre-2013 X250s.

problems.issue: Ingenium Diesel Timing Chain Tensioner Wear (Pre-2019)
problems.description: The AJ200D's timing chain tensioner can fail on pre-2019 cars, with cold-start rattle as the primary warning. JLR revised the design from 2019 onward with a marked reliability improvement.

problems.issue: AJ126 Pre-2013 Timing Chain Tensioner Wear
problems.description: The 3.0 V6's chain tensioner design was revised in 2013 — pre-2013 units carry a well-documented risk of tensioner failure, similar to the larger AJ133 V8.

problems.issue: AJ126 Supercharger Coupler / Torsion Isolator Rattle
problems.description: A rattly supercharger on the AJ126 V6 is a known shared quirk of the AJ126 and AJ133 — rarely catastrophic on its own but an annoyance and a sign of wear.
The petrol Ingenium's water pump is the primary weak point — replacement at 60k as preventative is recommended. Less costly than the diesel's timing chain failure.
Shared across the JLR range — mechatronic unit and torque converter issues are the most common cause. A genuine non-engine enquiry, but worth flagging.

marketIntelligence.h2: XF Market Intelligence — What 454+ UK Owners Told Us in 2025
insights: Interesting insight: The XF X260 generates 55% more enquiries than the older X250 — suggesting buyers and owners are actively researching the newer generation's Ingenium range, not just chasing cheap X250s.

The XF is Jaguar's #2 most-enquired model overall (XF X260 276 + XF X250 178 = 454 total), behind only the XE X760 (260) when summing both XF generations. This places the XF family as the second most-researched Jaguar in our dataset.

The gap between X260 and X250 enquiries reflects a genuine market shift — buyers are paying a premium for the Ingenium's improved reliability profile, and the data suggests the market has already priced the X250's AJ126 caveat into its value.

editorialPullQuote.title: The One Thing We'd Tell Every XF Buyer
editorialPullQuote.quote: If you buy one XF based on this page, buy a 2019+ X260 with the AJ200P petrol Ingenium — it's the cleanest reliability profile in the range, replacement costs are well within vehicle value, and you avoid the pre-2019 diesel timing chain caveat entirely. END OF PROMPT 1 OUTPUT — SECTIONS 1-7 COMPLETE JAGUAR XF — SECTIONS 8-14 Classification: Which Generation/Engine (carried forward from Prompt 1) Verified generation list: X250 (2008-2015), X260 (2015-2024) Verified engine list: AJ126, 276DT, 306DT, AJ200D/204DTD, AJ200P/204PT

replacementCosts.h2: XF Engine Replacement Costs — By Generation
replacementCosts.subHeadline: Real UK pricing for used, reconditioned and rebuilt XF engines — supply-only with labour guidance, sourced from actual Jaguar specialist quotes [JAG-QUOTE].
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Labour estimate: Fitting an XF engine typically takes 10-20 hours depending on variant at £65-£95/hr (independent) or £110-£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [JAG-QUOTE].
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an XF engine typically takes 10-20 hours depending on variant at £65-£95/hr (independent) or £110-£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [JAG-QUOTE].

engineEvolution.h2: How the XF Engine Range Evolved — 2008 to Today

eras.whyBmwChanged: Jaguar's Ford-era engine supply ended with the X250 platform — the AJ126 and TDV6 diesels were carried over from earlier S-Type and XJ models, but the cost base wasn't competitive with newer modular designs.
eras.worthKnowing: The 276DT oil pump housing failure is shared with Land Rover's Discovery 3 — same engine, same catastrophic risk.

eras.keyEngines: AJ200D 2.0 Ingenium Diesel, AJ200P 2.0 Ingenium Petrol
eras.whyBmwChanged: JLR's new Ingenium family was designed in-house to replace older Ford-derived units — better emissions compliance, modular architecture, and lower production costs.
eras.worthKnowing: Early diesel Ingenium (pre-2019) carried timing chain tensioner issues — JLR revised the design from 2019 onward. The petrol has always been the safer choice.

eras.whyBmwChanged: Timing chain tensioner redesign addressed the diesel's pre-2019 weakness. The revised units have a markedly cleaner reliability record.
eras.worthKnowing: The post-2019 diesel is the engine the X260 should have launched with — it's the reason we recommend 2019+ cars.

profiles.verdict: The X260 makes a superb family saloon — the estate (Sportbrake) adds useful load space, the Ingenium diesel returns strong economy, and replacement costs are well within the car's value. Avoid the X250 if carrying kids means carrying risk.

The X260 with the AJ200P petrol is the commuter's choice — refined, reliable, and the water pump is the only meaningful weak point. The diesel is fine if you're doing motorway miles, but the petrol has the cleaner reliability record in our data.

The X250 looks tempting at £4k-£7k, but the AJ126 V6's cooling system and chain tensioner risks mean you could be buying a £12k bill on a £5k car. If you're on a tight budget, walk away and buy an X260 with higher miles instead.

The X250's AJ126 3.0 V6 Supercharged offers genuine performance — 340PS and a supercharged soundtrack. But the cooling system needs constant monitoring, and the pre-2013 chain tensioner is a genuine risk. Buy a 2013+ car with documented cooling system work.

The X260 with the AJ200P petrol is the keeper's choice — the cleanest reliability profile in the XF range, replacement costs are sensible, and the Ingenium is a genuinely modern engine family with a long service life. Buy 2019+ for the revised components.

calculatorCta.intro: Timing chain rattle on an Ingenium diesel? Coolant system failure on an AJ126 V6? We've seen every XF failure pattern in our data — and we know exactly when to repair, when to replace, and when to walk away.

signals.title: Built on Real Data
signals.text: Every reliability rating and cost range on this page comes from 454+ real UK XF enquiries (2025), not manufacturer marketing or forum guesswork [JAG-VERIFIED].

signals.title: We Tell You When Not to Repair
signals.text: The X250's AJ126 recon can approach 60-80% of vehicle value on sub-£7k examples. We say so. That's the difference between a knowledge base and a sales page.
